name: Poem: "Advice from a Tree" — Ilan Shamir

sourceRefs.label: civilserviceaspirants.in - dedicated MCQ set, idiom-meaning focused

researchNotes: OCR index search for 'advice from a tree'/'ilan shamir' returned zero hits — web research only. Raw pool of 6 sourced MCQs, all kept — note this poem's question style leans heavily on idiom/phrase MEANING ('go out on a limb', 'stand tall and proud') rather than figure-of-speech labelling, a different testing emphasis than most other poems covered so far.

reading.intro: A poem using a tree's life as an extended metaphor for life advice — each tree-related image (roots, limbs, seasons, leaves) maps to a piece of wisdom. Unlike most other poems in this syllabus, real exam evidence here favours idiom/phrase MEANING questions over figure-of-speech labelling — know what each phrase means, not just what device it is.

qa.question: Who wrote "Advice from a Tree", and what's notable about him?
qa.answer: Ilan Shamir — notable for having planted over 50,000 trees and led more than 800 tree-planting celebrations worldwide, directly living out the nature-connection theme of his own poem. (He also wrote "The River", elsewhere in this same syllabus.)

qa.question: What is the poem's central theme?
qa.answer: Nature as a guide for living — using a tree's life and growth as a metaphor for how to live well.

qa.question: What does "Stand tall and proud" mean in the poem?
qa.answer: Have confidence and self-respect — not arrogance or literal height, but quiet inner confidence.

qa.question: What does "Sink your roots deeply into the Earth" mean?
qa.answer: Stay grounded and stable — having a firm foundation/values to rely on, just as a tree's roots anchor and stabilize it.

qa.question: What does "Go out on a limb" mean?
qa.answer: Take a risk — a common English idiom (literally, climbing onto a tree branch that might not hold your weight) used here for stepping beyond your comfort zone.

qa.question: What do the poem's seasons represent, and what does "the wisdom to let go of leaves in the Fall" symbolize?
qa.answer: The seasons represent stages and lessons of life. Letting go of leaves in Fall symbolizes letting go of burdens or attachments — knowing when to release what no longer serves you, just as a tree sheds leaves before winter.

questions.prompt: "Advice from a Tree" was written by:
The central theme of the poem is:
options: Nature as a guide for living
questions.explanation: Nature as a guide for living.

questions.prompt: In the poem, "Stand tall and proud" means:
"Sink your roots deeply into the Earth" means:

options: Sit on a tree branch literally
questions.explanation: Take a risk — a common idiom.

questions.prompt: "The wisdom to let go of leaves in the Fall" symbolizes:
options: Letting go of burdens or attachments
questions.explanation: Letting go of burdens or attachments.
